statement.summary: In this paper we develop the formalism of rational complex Bézier curves. This framework is a simple extension of the CAD paradigm, since it describes arcs of curves in terms of control polygons and weights, which are extended to complex values. One of the major advantages of this extension is that we may make use of two different groups of projective transformations. Besides the group of projective transformations of the real plane, we have the group of complex projective transformations. This allows us to apply useful transformations like the geometric inversion to curves in design. In addition to this, the use of the complex formulation allows to lower the degree of the curves in some cases. This can be checked using the resultant of two polynomials and provides a simple formula for det

proposer: Alicia Cantón, Leonardo Fernández Jambrina, María Jesús Vázquez Gallo

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Rational complex Bézier curves are a mathematically well-defined extension of established CAD theory. The framework builds on proven concepts (rational Bézier curves, complex projective geometry, control polygons) through rigorous algebraic formalism. The claims about projective transformation groups and degree reduction via polynomial resultants are verifiable within pure mathematics. This is a theoretical contribution with sound mathematical foundations.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 重みを持つ複素数の支配は、実と虚の二つの世界を同時に統治する。
